text:
Large UAE businesses have until 1 January 2027 to go live on FTA e-invoicing. The ones treating it as a 2027 problem have already lost.

Here's what most Dubai finance teams still get wrong:

- A PDF invoice is no longer a valid tax invoice. Only structured XML through an Accredited Service Provider counts.
- AED 50M+ firms must appoint an ASP by 30 October 2026.
- Non-compliance penalties run up to AED 5,000 per month.

The teams that win are consolidating onto Odoo 19, where e-invoicing, VAT, and audit-ready reporting live in one engine.

Comment AUDIT and I'll send our 7-point readiness checklist.

#OdooDubai #UAEeInvoicing #FTA #ERPImplementation #VATCompliance #Odoo19
alt: UAE e-invoicing timeline: pilot Jul 2026, ASP Oct 2026, go-live Jan 2027

text:
QuickBooks didn't fail you. You succeeded past it.

The tool that ran a 5-person startup quietly becomes the bottleneck at 50 - and most founders notice only when the monthly close starts eating a full week.

Signs you've outgrown entry-level accounting software:

- Your ERP is actually QuickBooks plus 4 spreadsheets plus a WhatsApp group.
- Inventory and accounting disagree, and nobody trusts either.
- FTA e-invoicing readiness is a question you keep postponing.

The leap isn't to enterprise bloat. It's to one platform that scales without a per-seat tax.

Comment SCALE for our diagnostic.

#SMEDubai #BusinessGrowth #ERP #Odoo #DubaiBusiness
